description: claude-code agent terminates with stop_reason=end_turn while a long-running command (bun install, Monitor poll, background bash) is still in flight. The model emits a natural-language assistant message like 'I'll wait for the install to finish' with no tool call; claude-code's run loop reads that as turn-complete and returns to the operator. In warren there is no operator to send the next prompt, so the run reaps before commit/push/finalize ever runs. Witnessed in run_a98cfx1fantf seq:105-106, compounded with warren-f3bb's silent-empty-branch path. Symptom: result subtype=success is_error=false but workspace branch has zero new commits because the agent self-described as mid-task.
resolution: Updated canopy claude-code prompt (canopy-daf3) v4 with explicit Expectations bullet: 'Never end your turn with work in flight. ... Polling output is not the same as done.' Cheap, narrow, fixes the witnessed instance. For other runtimes hitting variants of this seam, file a separate seed before generalizing — warren-side detection (B) and burrow-side wait-on-subprocess (C) are speculative and were deferred.
